role_title: People Experience Program Manager - San Francisco

location.office_time_required: in-person orientation components required; Flex First policy otherwise

level_signals: 5+ years in program management or related HR function
Base pay range $141K-$149K consistent with mid-senior IC
Expected to independently manage vendor relationships and cross-functional programs end-to-end

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years in program management, learning & development, talent development, or a related people/HR function
Hands-on experience using AI tools in a professional context (we'll ask you about this!)
Demonstrated ability to manage cross-functional projects from inception to completion — including when the problem or solution isn't fully defined at the outset
Proven experience facilitating learning or orientation content for diverse audiences
Track record of managing vendor relationships end-to-end: procurement, contract renewals, and ongoing partnership management
Located in the SF Bay Area with ability to support in-person orientation components in our San Francisco office

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree or equivalent professional experience
Experience with instructional design tools and/or learning management systems
Certification in project management (PMP, CAPM, or equivalent)
Experience applying AI to automate or redesign people programs at scale

scope.surface: New hire orientation and performance review cycle programs within the People Experience team
scope.business_model: Internal HR/People function at a grocery delivery marketplace (B2C/B2B)
primary_metrics_inferred: Orientation session operational quality and new hire satisfaction
Performance review cycle on-time completion and stakeholder alignment
core_responsibilities: Run new hire orientation logistics end-to-end with vendor management and continuous improvement
Execute performance review cycles with timeline management and stakeholder communications
Document, optimize, and automate manual processes across high-visibility people programs
Manage vendor relationships including procurement, contracts, SOWs, and budget tracking
Facilitate virtual and in-person orientation components and contribute to content iteration

compensation.equity_form: new hire equity grant and annual refresh grants

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($141K-$149K base) and competitive for SF
Equity grant (new hire and annual refresh) explicitly mentioned
Clear scope and first-year deliverables articulated in concrete terms
Flex First policy allows flexible work arrangement with in-person as needed
Role has direct, visible impact on two high-profile employee lifecycle moments
AI tool fluency explicitly valued — signals a forward-thinking team
red_flags: Hard requirement to be located in SF Bay Area with in-person obligations — eliminates remote candidates
Role scope is operationally heavy with facilitation expectations that may expand over time ('increasingly contributing to content iteration') without clear career ladder described
Compensation range is narrow ($8K spread) leaving little room for negotiation

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required and compensation range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from first-year deliverables; not explicitly stated as metrics)
ambiguities: JD describes the company as 'Flex First' (work from anywhere) but the role requires SF Bay Area location and in-person orientation support — effectively hybrid/onsite for this role. Treated as hybrid.

The role title includes 'San Francisco' which implies onsite obligation, but Flex First policy language suggests flexibility outside of orientation days. The degree of in-office requirement beyond orientation is unspecified.
